release_review.summary: Runtime update flows now verify the running Runtime Service version after restart, persist self-upgrade maintenance reconciliation across exec, and expose clearer Desktop-managed SSH mismatch diagnostics.
checked_surfaces: sys.ping maintenance snapshots add optional cross-exec reconciliation diagnostics including previous/observed runtime instance ids while preserving existing kind, state, target_version, message, and timestamp fields
Env App maintenance polling treats Updated as successful only after sys.ping or Runtime Service identity reaches the requested release tag
Runtime-owned self-upgrade writes a local maintenance marker before installer execution and reconciles it during the next process startup
Desktop-managed SSH startup keeps the existing machine startup report contract and adds non-secret expected/observed version diagnostics for identity mismatch failures
Container launcher update continues to stop and verify the runtime daemon before package replacement and startup
Local UI health, startup reports, runtime-control, provider link, and desktop bridge protocol fields keep the same compatibility window
release_review.same_window_rationale: The release adds optional diagnostic fields to sys.ping maintenance snapshots and tightens client-side success semantics, including per-attempt filtering for persisted maintenance snapshots, without removing or changing any existing Runtime Service JSON fields, protocol version, runtime-control endpoint, startup report shape, Local UI health surface, provider-link surface, or desktop bridge framing. Older clients can ignore the new optional fields, and newer clients still receive the existing maintenance kind/state/message data from older runtimes.
